text: Their crab cake is the benchmark. Dense, minimal filler, all crab. Worth the Lexington Market trip every time.
Came back for the fourth time this year. Consistency is impressive — same quality as my first visit.
Great crabs, good atmosphere, a bit loud for a solo lunch but the blue crabs are seasoned right.
Solid spot. Picnic table vibe outside. Not a quiet dinner option but good for a summer afternoon.
Best soul food in the city. Quiet atmosphere, attentive staff, cornbread is exceptional. The fried catfish plate is my standard order.
Took my parents here on a Sunday. Mother approved, which is a high bar. Collard greens on point.
Good brunch spot. Not too loud on weekdays. Shrimp and grits are well-executed. Skip weekend mornings if noise matters to you.
Quiet, well-lit, excellent sourcing. The oysters change seasonally. Reservations recommended. A real treat for a non-deadline night out.
Unpretentious, genuinely good Southern cooking. Quiet enough to bring a book. Mac and cheese is the real deal.
Came back on a cold January Tuesday. Never crowded, always consistent. Exactly what I needed mid-manuscript.
Nice spot close to Charles Village. Crab cake is solid — not Faidley's level but a convenient option. Quiet weekday lunches.
Location is great, Inner Harbor views nice. A bit corporate-feeling and the noise level at dinner is not for me. Stick to lunch.
Classic Baltimore crab house. Seasonal, unpretentious, good spice on the seasoning. Gets busy Friday nights.
Neighborhood gem. Rotating specials, calm vibe, genuinely friendly service. The baked mac is a standout.
Reliable weeknight dinner. Smaller menu means everything is done well. Good enough for the third time in six months.
Lunch special is a deal. Pork chop with two sides for under $15. Nothing fancy, comfortable seating, low noise.
Good Hampden spot. Sunday brunch crowd is manageable. Fried chicken biscuit is excellent. Parking is the one issue.
Quick crab cake fix at Lexington Market. Cash-only stall, busy but fast. Good when I want the real thing without a sit-down.
Decent lunches, inconsistent on my last two visits. The oxtail was good once, unremarkable the second time. Worth watching.
Waterfront setting is a plus. Crab cakes are middle-of-the-road but the soft shells in season are excellent.
Views are wonderful, prices match the real estate. Food is acceptable, service slow. A once-a-year destination at most.
